finding: User spent time across two sessions investigating disk space issues — identifying pip cache, stale venvs, ONNX model exports, and old ISOs as major consumers. The goal (free disk space from accumulated caches) recurs naturally as development tools generate debris over time. A cron script or systemd timer that periodically prunes known cache directories (pip, venv, huggingface, build artifacts) could eliminate this manual housekeeping.

User attempted to connect Kindle to Calibre and said 'I know you solved this problem for me before' — indicating this udev rule fix has been done at least twice. The session involved switching models, trying multiple approaches, and manual sudo commands. The fix (a udev rule at /etc/udev/rules.d/) should persist, but something causes it to be lost — possibly system updates or NixOS rebuilds overwriting it. Capturing this in the NixOS config or a dotfiles-tracked udev rules directory would solve it once-and-for-all.
existing_mitigation: None found. The udev rule was manually recreated but the persistence mechanism is unclear.

finding: User ran a manual drift analysis session comparing live Mint gsettings, installed packages, systemd units, and dotfiles against the NixOS config to find what would be lost on a fresh install. User explicitly said 'I keep doing things to mint. How can I make sure these changes are mirrored?' and asked for proposal files per finding. This is a recurring concern — the gap between live system state and declarative config grows daily. A scheduled drift scan (cron/systemd timer) that compares live state to NixOS config and generates per-finding proposals would catch drift automatically.
existing_mitigation: User started building a proposal-based workflow in the nixos-config repo during this session, but no automated scheduled scan exists yet.

finding: After optimizing whisper-writer transcription speed, user explicitly requested 'an automated, cron-job based Claude instance that looks for updates and tells me if there are better options available' and created a proposals folder for it. The user self-identified this automation need — monitoring for better transcription models is a recurring concern as the ML ecosystem evolves. The proposal was filed but not yet implemented.
existing_mitigation: A proposals folder was created in the whisper-writer repo with a description of the desired automation, but no implementation exists yet.

finding: After pushing changes to the Autodoro repo, user manually asked to 'reload the service' and then immediately said 'actually, add onpush hook that reloads the service'. This pattern of push-then-manual-reload recurs for any locally-deployed service. User then discussed whether git hooks should be checked in. A post-push hook or a general deploy-on-push pattern would eliminate this step for all similar repos.
existing_mitigation: User requested and received a git hook during this session. The hook may now exist for Autodoro specifically, but no general cross-repo pattern is established.
